---
title: "LST_NVDI_Correlation"
output: html_document
---

# Libraries

```{r}

# Libraries
library(terra)
library(tidyverse)
library(viridis)

```

# LST vs NVDI Correlation

```{r}

region <- c("Senegal", "Chad")

# Function to create correlation data
create_correlation_data <- function(region) {
  # Define paths
  ndvi_path <- file.path("Data", region, "Vegetation", "NDVI")
  lst_path  <- file.path("Data", region, "Temperature", "Day")
  
  # Get August files for both
  ndvi_files <- list.files(ndvi_path, pattern = "\\.08\\.tif$", full.names = TRUE)
  lst_files  <- list.files(lst_path, pattern = "\\.08\\.tif$", full.names = TRUE)
  
  corr_list <- list()
  
  for (i in seq_along(ndvi_files)) {
    year <- stringr::str_extract(basename(ndvi_files[i]), "\\d{4}")
    
    # Load both rasters
    r_ndvi <- rast(ndvi_files[i])
    r_lst  <- rast(lst_files[i])
    
    # Ensure they have the exact same extent/resolution
    r_lst <- resample(r_lst, r_ndvi)
    
    # Convert to DF and merge
    df_ndvi <- as.data.frame(r_ndvi, xy = TRUE)
    df_lst  <- as.data.frame(r_lst, xy = TRUE)
    
    colnames(df_ndvi)[3] <- "NDVI"
    colnames(df_lst)[3]  <- "LST_K" # MODIS LST is usually in Kelvin
    
    # Join by coordinates and convert Kelvin to Celsius
    combined <- inner_join(df_ndvi, df_lst, by = c("x", "y")) %>%
      mutate(LST_C = LST_K - 273.15,
             Year = as.numeric(year),
             Region = region) %>%
      filter(NDVI > 0) # Remove water/noise
    
    # To keep the scatterplot from having 1 million points (which crashes R), 
    # we take a random sample of 5000 points per year
    corr_list[[year]] <- combined %>% sample_n(min(nrow(combined), 5000))
  }
  
  return(bind_rows(corr_list))
}

senegal_corr <- create_correlation_data("Senegal")
chad_corr    <- create_correlation_data("Chad")

```

```{r}

# Triangle plot
ggplot(senegal_corr, aes(x = LST_C, y = NDVI)) +
  geom_bin2d(bins = 100) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma", name = "Point Density") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "cyan", linetype = "dashed") + # "Evaporative Cooling" slope
  
  labs(title = "LST vs. NDVI Correlation: Senegal",
       subtitle = "Top-Left Corner = Desertified 'Hot-Dry' Zone",
       x = "Land Surface Temperature (°C)",
       y = "Vegetation Index (NDVI)") +
  theme_bw()

ggplot(chad_corr, aes(x = LST_C, y = NDVI)) +
  geom_bin2d(bins = 100) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma", name = "Point Density") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "cyan", linetype = "dashed") + # "Evaporative Cooling" slope
  
  labs(title = "LST vs. NDVI Correlation: Chad",
       subtitle = "Top-Left Corner = Desertified 'Hot-Dry' Zone",
       x = "Land Surface Temperature (°C)",
       y = "Vegetation Index (NDVI)") +
  theme_bw()

```

```{r}

# Merged correlations
master_corr_df <- bind_rows(senegal_corr, chad_corr)

master_corr_df$Region <- factor(master_corr_df$Region, levels = c("Senegal", "Chad"))

```

```{r}

ggplot(master_corr_df, aes(x = LST_C, y = NDVI)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.05, size = 0.5, color = "grey30") +
  geom_density_2d(aes(color = ..level..), linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis(option = "viridis", name = "Density Level") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~Region, scales = "fixed") + 
  labs(
    title = "Thermal-Vegetation Correlation: Senegal vs. Chad",
    subtitle = "Higher LST at low NDVI indicates desertification",
    x = "Land Surface Temperature (°C)",
    y = "Vegetation Index (NDVI)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1))

```

```{r}

border_lats <- master_hov_df %>%
  filter(Mean_NDVI >= 0.19 & Mean_NDVI <= 0.21) %>%
  group_by(Region) %>%
  summarise(Avg_Border_Lat = mean(Latitude_Slice, na.rm = TRUE))

master_corr_df <- master_corr_df %>%
  left_join(border_lats, by = "Region") %>%
  # Keep only data within 3 degrees of the Sahara border
  filter(y >= (Avg_Border_Lat - 3) & y <= (Avg_Border_Lat + 3))

```
